mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to automate cluster lifecycle management across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installation.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all the CRDs needed for Cluster API.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components into the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Ensure you have the kube-system namespace created.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to manage your Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  controlPlane:
    ref:
      apiVersion: controlplane.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
      kind: KubeadmControlPlane
      name: my-control-plane
EOF

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your desired cluster name.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  controlPlane:
    ref:
      apiVersion: controlplane.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
      kind: KubeadmControlPlane
      name: my-control-plane
EOF

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see Cluster API components running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API components
uninstall.description:
This step removes Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this may lead to data loss for managed clusters.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This will remove all CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster resources. This step will upgrade Cluster API to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kube-system -o yaml > backup-cluster-api.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup current Cluster API resources.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.
commands: kubectl get all -n kube-system -o yaml > backup-cluster-api.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: control plane is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the control plane components are not running or have failed.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the control plane pods with: kubectl get pods -n kube-system and troubleshoot any failed pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a problem with the kubeconfig or the API server certificate.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig is correctly configured and that the API server is reachable.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found in kube-system namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API components are not installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Cluster API components using the Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved the stability of existing components.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old API versions for Cluster and Machine resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your custom resources to the new API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kube-system' showing all Cluster API components in a Running state.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  controlPlane:
    ref:
      apiVersion: controlplane.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
      kind: KubeadmControlPlane
      name: my-control-plane
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
